stories.headline: Nomura's Koo: Weak Yen Tied to Slow BOJ Hikes
stories.summary: Nomura Research Institute Chief Economist Richard Koo, widely recognized for his work on balance sheet recessions and global macroeconomic trends, believes the fundamental cause of a weak yen is the slow pace of rate hikes by the Bank of Japan. He speaks with Yvonne Man and Minmin Low from the sidelines of 'Nomura Investment Forum Asia' in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Chinese Firm Seeks $2.6 Billion Loan for Hong Kong Data Center
stories.summary: Chinese data center operator Range Intelligent Computing Technology Group Co. is seeking a loan of around HK$20 billion ($2.6 billion) to fund what would be Hong Kong’s largest computing facility, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: B&M Ignites Short Squeeze as Stock Heads For Best Ever Week
stories.summary: A record rally in B&M European Value Retail Plc has squeezed short sellers betting against the stock, after the UK discounter’s better-than-expected earnings bolstered confidence in its turnaround effort.

stories.headline: AI Trade Cools as Asia Rotates and Indonesia Cracks | Insight with Haslinda Amin 06/05/2026
stories.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: How Indian Equities Could Thrive amid Market Gloom
stories.summary: Deeply negative sentiment may be setting the stage for a recovery in Indian equities, according to Devina Mehra, Chairperson and MD of First Global. She argues that widespread pessimism and heightened focus on risks historically improve the probability of stronger market returns, while valuations are not as elevated as commonly perceived. She spoke with Menaka Doshi on Bloomberg's Insight with Haslinda Amin (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ‘Sell Indonesia’ Sweeps Trading Desks as Prabowo Tightens Grip

stories.headline: India Scraps Taxes to Attract Foreign Investment in Bonds
stories.summary: India scrapped taxes and removed caps on the ownership of some bonds for foreign investors to attract overseas capital and stabilize the rupee, which has been under pressure from higher energy prices and equity market outflows.

stories.headline: India Central Bank Takes Steps to Boost Rupee, Holds Rate

stories.headline: Korea Leads Drop as AI Mania Gets Tested | The Asia Trade 6/5/2026

(Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: India Said to Weigh Up to $525 Million Hindustan Zinc Share Sale
